text.title: Partnership Co-Marketing Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Partnership Co-Marketing Playbook MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Partnership MUST be backed by >=60% ICP overlap (named segment match). Below threshold, partnership wastes both audiences.
rule.rationale: Low-overlap partnerships generate 5-10x worse leads; both sides lose audience trust.

rule.statement: Audience sizes MUST be within 3x of each other (e.g., 1k vs. 3k OK, 1k vs. 10k not). Asymmetric audiences collapse the deal.
rule.rationale: Larger-audience partner sees smaller as freeloader; expectations misalign; partnership ends bitter.

rule.statement: Spec MUST include shared UTM + post-event survey + lead-attribution split. Un-attributed partnerships cannot be evaluated.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, both sides assume the other got more value; no second collaboration.

rule.statement: Each partnership MUST commit to exactly one joint asset (webinar OR report OR bundle) — not bundles of bundles.
rule.rationale: Multi-asset partnerships over-extend both teams; success rate drops 60%.

rule.statement: Spec MUST list exit criteria (lead-quality threshold, audience growth, re-collaboration trigger). Indefinite partnerships are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without exits, dead partnerships consume calendar slots that could fund new ones.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
